evidence: Each frame has a single load-bearing element: frame 1 the folding qualities, frame 3 the lone c1 score bar, frames 5-6 the parked probe with the four landed bars reading as supporting rows.
The probe, four candidate arrows, and four score bars are introduced progressively across the build (2 arrows in frame 2, four plus bars later), never five-plus novel ideas at once.
Labels 'probe', c1-c4 sit directly beside their arrows and each score numeric sits on its own bar; no legend or corner key appears.
Only labels and numeric readouts (timbre: 0.9, +4.7, etc.) appear, each annotating a visible arrow or bar; no free-floating prose blocks.
All labels and mono numerics are rendered at a comfortably readable size well above the ~2.2% frame-height guide.
Arrows, bars, and labels all sit inside the frame with margins; nothing is cropped by an edge.
In frames 2-3 the 'c1' label and clustered arrowheads sit close but remain separable, and no text element is stamped illegibly across another.
The probe stays amber (attention), candidate arrows periwinkle (observed), and score bars lilac (parameters/measure) consistently across all frames.
Every element — arrows, labels, bars, numerics — carries propositional content; no mascots or ornaments appear.

The qualities fold to a single arrow, an amber probe sweeps while four periwinkle candidates hold, and lilac score bars climb/land as numerics — the promised composition is on screen, though the amber arrowhead in frames 2-3 reads slightly bloated relative to its short shaft.
suggestion: Scale the amber probe arrowhead down to match the candidate arrows' head-to-shaft proportion when the shaft is short.

evidence: Each score numeric sits on its bar and each label touches its arrow, so claims stay next to their objects.
The sequence builds in reasoning order: qualities collapse to a dot, arrows appear one pair then all four, bars grow in, and the probe sweeps continuously to its parked pose.
The diagram sits upper-center with the score row spread evenly below, leaving generous negative space and a clear top-to-bottom scan path.
